license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kw8cxhw
title: Flow Cytometry for Monocyte Phenotype and Mitochondrial Mass
author: Brandt D Pence

steps_raw.instruction: Collect blood in K2EDTA tube and store at room temperature until use.
Aliquot 100 μl whole blood into 12×75mm test tubes, 1 tube per subject.

Aliquot blood into control tubes (can use blood from one subject or mixed blood, 100 μl per tube) (a) Unstained control (b) CD14 only control (c) CD16 only control (d) Mitotracker only control (e) FMO (note 1) no CD14 (f) FMO no CD16 (g) FMO no Mitotracker FMO = fluorescence minus one control. Includes all stains except the one indicated (e.g. FMO no CD16 includes CD14 and Mitotracker stains, but not CD16 stain).
Add 10 μg IgG to each tube and incubate 15 minutes at 4degC-25degC to block Fc receptors

Add antibodies to tubes:CD14 antibody: 20 μl to all samples plus CD14 only control, FMO no CD16, FMO no MitotrackerCD16 antibody: 1 μl to all samples plus CD16 only control, FMO no CD14, FMO no Mitotracker
Vortex gently and incubate 1 hour in the dark at 4degC-25degC
Add 1 ml of lysing buffer, vortex gently and incubate in the dark at 4degC-25degC for 10 minutes.
While lysing, prepare MitotrackerDilute 1 μl stock in 999 μl stain bufferDilute 200 μl initial dilution in 800 μl stain buffer for final concentration of 200 nM
Centrifuge lysed blood at 500×g for 5 minutes. Remove supernatant.
Add 200 μl Mitotracker to all samples plus Mitotracker only control, FMO no CD14, FMO no CD16Add 200 μl staining buffer to unstained control, CD14 only, CD16 only, FMO no Mitotracker.
Incubate 1 hour in the dark at 4degC-25degC.
Add 1 ml staining buffer and centrifuge 500×g for 5 minutes. Remove supernatant.
Resuspend in 400 μl lysing buffer. Analyze as quickly as possible.

Data Analysis For the listed antibodies, a flow cytometer with blue (488 nm) and violet (405 nm) lasers is necessary. Adjust forward scatter, side scatter, and fluorescence detector sensitivity as appropriate. Gate based on unstained control and FMO controls with compensation based on single stain tubes. Gate monocyte population ( Figure 1A ) and show scatter plot of CD16 vs. CD14 ( Figure 1B ). Mitotracker data can be analyzed as median fluorescence intensity between samples or cell populations. a Figure 1. Typical flow cytometry results. (A) Typical forward and side scatter plot. Monocytes are gated. (B) Typical CD16 vs. CD14 plot. Monocytes are separated into classical (CD14+CD16-), intermediate (CD14+CD16+), and non-classical (CD14dimCD16+) subtypes.
Materials to Prepare Mitotracker Green FM Prepare as directed and store 1mM stock solution at -20 o C.
